name: RTLLM
notes: Repository README states that RTLLM 2.0 includes 50 designs.
Suitable as a curated open-source RTL benchmark source.

name: VerilogEval
notes: Main branch README describes VerilogEval v2 with a plain-text `dataset_spec-to-rtl` task set.
The official repository ships 156 spec-to-RTL problems and matching self-checking testbenches.

name: RealBench
notes: Repository README positions RealBench as a benchmark for complex real-world IP design tasks with syntax, function, and formal verification.
The benchmark documents are GPG-encrypted before use, so direct mirroring into this repo needs a separate review.

name: RTL-Repo
notes: Repository README describes more than 4000 real-world Verilog samples.
Best used for repository-context evaluation rather than single-module smoke tests.

name: PyHDL-Eval
notes: Official README describes 168 new problems spanning 19 RTL subclasses.
The public GitHub repo removes Verilog reference solutions, so this repo can catalog the source but cannot directly mirror complete RTL tasks from GitHub alone.

name: AutoBench
notes: README states the HDLBits subset in this repo is extended from HDLBits data, so redistribution of those derived samples should be reviewed separately before mirroring.

name: CorrectBench
notes: Repository README says the dataset in `data/HDLBits` is extended from HDLBits data.
Useful for testbench-generation and self-correction workflows, but redistribution should be reviewed before mirroring.
